deadlines.notes: Cannot e-file without registration. May take 1-3 business days.
Required for federal e-filing. Registration may take 1-5 business days.

deadlines.description: Review and sign all 10 affidavits (sworn under oath)
deadlines.notes: MUST be notarized or signed under penalty of perjury. Cannot file without signed affidavit.
Need income, expenses, bank balances, assets. See 09_IFP_APPLICATION.md in each package.

deadlines.description: Serve Emily Watson with F1 Emergency Motion
deadlines.notes: MCR 2.119(C) — motion must be served 9 days before hearing. Court may shorten for emergency.

deadlines.description: Serve Emily Watson with F2 Fraud Complaint + Summons
deadlines.notes: MCR 2.102(D) — summons expires after 91 days. Must perfect service before then.

deadlines.description: Judge McNeill response to disqualification motion
deadlines.notes: MCR 2.003(D)(3) — if judge does not recuse within 14 days, motion goes to chief judge.

deadlines.description: Serve ALL defendants in federal §1983 case
deadlines.notes: FRCP 4(m) — if not served within 90 days, case may be dismissed. Request US Marshals if IFP.

deadlines.description: Defendants must respond to federal complaint
deadlines.notes: FRCP 12(a)(1) — 21 days to answer or file motion to dismiss. State actors get 60 days (FRCP 12(a)(2)).
COA 366810 already docketed. Brief may already be due. CALL COA CLERK: (517) 373-0786 to confirm deadline.

deadlines.description: Serve proposed settled statement on Emily Watson
deadlines.notes: MCR 7.210(B)(2)(b) — opposing party has 14 days to object after service.

deadlines.description: Fraud relief from judgment — 1 year limit
deadlines.notes: MCR 2.612(C)(1)(c) has 1-year limit. BUT MCR 2.612(C)(3) independent fraud action has NO time limit. Use (C)(3).
Michigan borrows 3-year personal injury SOL for §1983. Continuing violations doctrine extends if pattern ongoing.
